sections.paragraph:
constr. st. סְרִיס, pl. סָרִיסִים const.סָרִיסֵי, סִרִיסֵי and Syr. ܣܳܪܝܺܣܐܳ-

(1) a eunuch, one castrated (see the root סָרַס ), Isa 56:3, Isa 56:4such as the Eastern kings were accustomed to set over the care of their women ( Est 2:3, 14 , 15 4:5 ), and other offices of the court ( Est 1:10, 12 , 15 ). רַב הַסָּרִיסִים Dan 1:3 שַׂר הַסָּרִיסִים verse , seq., “the prince of the eunuchs,” who was over the royal children, just as now in Turkey. Kislar Aga, the prince of the eunuchs, has the charge of the royal children of the Sultan, called Itshoghlan. Hence-

(2) any minister of the court, although not castrated ( Gen 37:6 39:1 ); although it is difficult to determine in what places the primary meaning of the word is preserved, and in what it is lost, 1Sa 8:15 1Ki 22:9 2Ki 9:32 20:18 24:12 , 15 2Ki 24:15 25:19 (where סָרִיס is a military leader); Jer 34:19 41:16 . Targum sometimes renders it רַבָּא a prince. Arab. حَدِمُ minister. The Syriac, however, always renders it ܡܗܰܝܡܢܐܳ a eunuch (prop. faithful, as eunuchs were considered remarkable for fidelity to their masters); and so LXX., Vulg.
